Privacy Promise

Welcome! We’re glad you’re here and want you to know that we respect your privacy and your right to control how we collect, use, and share your personal data. Please read our Privacy Policy to learn about our privacy practices.

    Summary (key insights in a concise summary) See more 
    
                        
    
                        
    
                                                       This AI-powered analysis provides insights into housing features and educational environments for properties in your search. It includes a breakdown of home amenities, highlighting features like Private Pool (1,040 homes), Cul-De-Sac locations (892 homes) and Golf communities (136 homes). Special home features detail the number of stories, with one and a half-story homes being the most common (74). Educational environment insights list nearby schools, such as Lamar High School associated with 1,345 homes and rated "B" and Wisdom High School associated with 889 homes and rated "B". 
                           
                                                       
    👉 Click on any pill to instantly filter and explore those listings.

    Seller Contribution
    
        
        
    
        
    
        
        
    
            
    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses
    Up To     .
    
                
            Contact Agent
                    
    
        
        
    
            
    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses. Explanation ↓
    
                                
        
    
    
    


    


    


    
    
    
    
    
        
    
        
    Seller Contribution
    
        
        
    
        
    
        
        
    
            
    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses
    Up To     .
    
                
            Contact Agent
                    
    
        
        
    
            
    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

            Contact Agent
                    
    
        
        
    
            
    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    Seller Contribution
    
        
        
    
        
    
        
        
    
            
    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses
    Up To     .
    
                
            Contact Agent
                    
    
        
        
    
            
    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses. Explanation ↓
    
                                
        
    
    
    


    


    


    
    
    
    
    
        
    
        
    Seller Contribution
    
        
        
    
        
    
        
        
    
            
    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses
    Up To     .
    
                
            Contact Agent
                    
    
        
        
    
            
    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    Seller Contribution
    
        
        
    
        
    
        
        
    
            
    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses
    Up To     .
    
                
            Contact Agent
                    
    
        
        
    
            
    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    1,134 Sqft. ($281/Sqft.)

    Seller Contribution
    
        
        
    
        
    
        
        
    
            
    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses
    Up To     .
    
                
            Contact Agent
                    
    
        
        
    
            
    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    Active
    
                                    
                                                                                                    
    
                    
    

            
                
                
    
                    For Sale, Single-Family
    
                                        Contemporary/Modern style in Combre Parkview Villas in Riverside (Marketarea)
                                        

                
    
                
    
                                                                   
    3 bedrooms
    
                                                                                                                   
    2,005 Sqft. ($170/Sqft.)

    Seller Contribution
    
        
        
    
        
    
        
        
    
            
    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses
    Up To     .
    
                
            Contact Agent
                    
    
        
        
    
            
    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    Seller Contribution
    
        
        
    
        
    
        
        
    
            
    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses
    Up To     .
    
                
            Contact Agent
                    
    
        
        
    
            
    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    Seller Contribution
    
        
        
    
        
    
        
        
    
            
    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses
    Up To     .
    
                
            Contact Agent
                    
    
        
        
    
            
    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    About Houston, Texas
    
      
    A Bustling City with a Rich History, a Vibrant Culture, and a Bright Future
    
      
    
         Located in Southeast Texas, Houston is the fourth-largest city in the United States, with a population of over 2.4 million people. Founded in 1836 by the Allen brothers, it has a rich history that has been shaped by its position as a hub for industry, commerce, and innovation. Due to its proximity to the Gulf of Mexico, it was envisioned as a major trading port and is today known as the “Energy Capital of the World” in the energy sector.
         

    
         Despite its industrial reputation, Houston is also famous for its diverse and dynamic culture, with a thriving art scene that includes a world-class museum district and a lively theater community. With residents from various ethnic and cultural backgrounds, the city is a melting pot of people and perspectives. The city also boasts a wide range of restaurants and food trucks serving up traditional Tex-Mex cuisine and international flavors. Being a unique blend of lavish amenities, a warm climate, and welcoming people, Houston is an exceptional place to visit or live.
      
    
      
    Amenities and Attractions – What Living in Houston, Texas, Offers
    
      
    Living in Houston offers a wide range of amenities and an exciting lifestyle. With a cosmopolitan character, the city caters to an eclectic mix of tastes and interests. Whether you are a quiet, introverted person who prefers to maintain a low profile or a boisterous socialite who enjoys frequenting bars and clubs, Houston has something for everyone.
         

    
         From state-of-the-art healthcare facilities and several prestigious universities and colleges to a strong job market and a relatively low cost of living compared to other major cities in the United States, Houston has a large number of attractions. 
      
    
      
      
    
         
            
    Things to Do
    
             
         
    
            
    There are plenty of things to do in Houston, Texas. Here are some of the most popular attractions and activities:
    
            
      
               
    • Visit NASA Space Center: Houston is home to the NASA Space Center, a top attraction where visitors can learn about space exploration and even see real spacecraft and astronauts.
      • 
               
    • Explore the Museum District: The Museum District is a must-visit destination for art and history enthusiasts, with over 19 museums and cultural institutions.
      • 
               
    • Enjoy a thrilling live performance at the Theater District: The Theater District in Houston is a vibrant cultural hub that boasts several performing arts venues and hosts a wide range of productions, including Broadway shows, musicals, ballets, operas, and concerts. 
               
      • 
               
    • Visit the Houston Zoo: The Houston Zoo is a popular family-friendly attraction, with over 6,000 animals representing over 900 species.
      • 
               
    • Take a stroll in Buffalo Bayou Park: Buffalo Bayou Park is a beautiful green space with hiking and biking trails, kayaking and paddle boarding opportunities, and stunning views of the Houston skyline.
      • 
               
    • Shop at The Galleria:  The Galleria is one of the largest shopping malls in the United States, with over 400 stores and restaurants.
      • 
               
    • Visit the Houston Museum of Natural Science: The Houston Museum of Natural Science is a fascinating destination with exhibits on dinosaurs, ancient Egypt, and space exploration.
      • 
               
    • Watch a baseball game at Minute Maid Park: Minute Maid Park is a popular destination for baseball fans and is an iconic part of the Houston skyline.
      • 
               
    • Enjoy the food scene: Houston is known for its diverse and delicious food scene, from Tex-Mex and BBQ to international cuisine.
      • 
               
    • Experience the excitement of the Houston Livestock Show and Rodeo: The Houston Livestock Show and Rodeo, also called RodeoHouston, is an annual event featuring various activities such as bull riding, barrel racing, and livestock shows.
      • 
            
    
            
    Offering all these wonderful experiences and many more, Houston is an exciting place to explore and enjoy.
    
         
    
      
    
      
      
      
    
         
            
    Schools and
               Educational Opportunities
            
    
             
         
    
            
    
               Houston has a comprehensive educational system that includes public and private schools, as well as higher learning institutions. The city’s largest school district is the Houston Independent School District, while private schools offer different educational philosophies and curricula. Houston is also home to several esteemed colleges and universities, which provide undergraduate and graduate programs in various fields, including business, engineering, medicine, and law.
               

    
               Moreover, Houston has several community colleges and vocational schools that provide technical training and certification programs. The city also has an active adult education program that offers classes and training for those seeking to improve their skills or learn something new. These educational opportunities cater to a wide range of interests and career paths, making Houston a great place for students to pursue their education and career goals.
               

    
               Here are some of the top educational resources within and near Houston, Texas:
            
    
            
    Elementary School(s):
    
            
      
               
    • River Oaks Elementary School
      • 
               
    • Memorial Elementary School
      • 
               
    • Poe Elementary School
      • 
               
    • Horn Elementary School
      • 
            
    
            
    Middle School(s):
    
            
      
               
    • Lanier Middle School
      • 
               
    • Pin Oak Middle School
      • 
               
    • Tanglewood Middle School
      • 
            
    
            
    High School(s):
    
            
      
               
    • Lamar High School
      • 
               
    • Bellaire High School
      • 
               
    • Carnegie Vanguard High School
      • 
            
    
            
    Institution(s) of Higher Learning:
    
            
      
               
    • University of Houston
      • 
               
    • Rice University
      • 
               
    • Texas Southern University
      • 
               
    • Houston Community College System
      • 
               
    • Lone Star College System
      • 
               
    • San Jacinto College
      • 
               
    • Alvin Community College
      • 
               
    • Blinn College
      • 
               
    • Brazosport College
      • 
               
    • College of the Mainland
      • 
               
    • Lee College
      • 
               
    • Wharton County Junior College
      • 
            
    
         
    
      
    
      
      
      
    
         
            
    Healthcare and Pharmaceutical Facilities
    
             
         
    
            
    
               Houston is home to several world-renowned medical institutions, making it a hub for healthcare and pharmaceuticals. The Texas Medical Center, located in the city, is the largest medical center in the world, with over 50 institutions and hospitals. These institutions offer a wide range of medical services, including research, education, and patient care.
               

    
               The medical institutions in Houston attract some of the top medical professionals from around the world, creating a highly skilled workforce. The city is also home to several pharmaceutical companies, including Johnson & Johnson and Merck & Co., which have research and development facilities in the area. With a booming healthcare and pharmaceutical industry, Houston offers excellent opportunities for those seeking careers in these fields.
            
    
            
    Major Hospitals in Houston, Texas:
    
            
      
               
    • Houston Methodist Hospital:  Located in the Texas Medical Center, it’s one of the largest and most renowned hospitals in Houston.
      • 
               
    • Texas Children’s Hospital: A world-famous pediatric hospital located in the Texas Medical Center.
      • 
               
    • Memorial Hermann-Texas Medical Center:
                  Another major hospital in the Texas Medical Center known for its comprehensive services.
               
      • 
               
    • St. Luke’s Health System: Operates several hospitals across the Houston area, including St. Luke’s Health–The Woodlands Hospital.
      • 
            
    
            
    Pharmaceutical and Healthcare Chains:
    
            
      
               
    • Houston Methodist Hospital:  Located in the Texas Medical Center, it’s one of the largest and most renowned hospitals in Houston.
      • 
               
    • Walgreens: A well-known pharmacy chain with multiple locations in Houston.
      • 
               
    • CVS Pharmacy: Another popular pharmacy chain with numerous branches in the city.
      • 
               
    • Houston Healthcare Clinics: There are various healthcare clinics and urgent care centers across the city, often affiliated with larger hospital systems.
      • 
            
    
         
    
      
    
      
      
      
    
         
            
    Business and Employment Opportunities
    
             
         
    
            
    
               Houston is a flourishing city with a diverse economy offering attractive prospects. While its strong presence in the energy sector is its major economic driver, its economy is supported by various other industries such as healthcare, aerospace, and technology. Being home to several Fortune 500 companies, it provides a plethora of job opportunities for both entry-level and experienced professionals.
               

    
               In addition to large corporations, Houston has a thriving entrepreneurial community, with numerous start-ups and small businesses. The city offers resources and support for entrepreneurs and small business owners, such as co-working spaces, accelerators, and funding opportunities. Overall, Houston’s business and job opportunities attract professionals from various industries. The city’s strong economy, large corporations, and entrepreneurial community make it a great place to seek career aspirations.  
               

    
               
    Major employers in Houston, Texas, include:
    
            
    
            
      
               
    • ConocoPhillips
      • 
               
    • Phillips 66
      • 
               
    • Sysco Corporation
      • 
               
    • The Texas Medical Center
      • 
               
    • ExxonMobil
      • 
               
    • Chevron
      • 
               
    • Shell Oil Company
      • 
               
    • United Airlines
      • 
               
    • NASA Johnson Space Center
      • 
               
    • University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer
                  Center
               
      • 
               
    • Houston Independent School District (HISD)
               
      • 
            
    
         
    
      
    
      
      
    Real Estate – What It Is Like Living in Houston, Texas
    
      
    Living in Houston, Texas provides an extensive and varied real estate experience, accommodating a diverse range of housing needs and preferences.
    
      
    Property Types
    
      
    Houston’s real estate market is incredibly diverse, catering to a wide range of needs and preferences. Residential options include single-family homes, townhouses, apartments, and condominiums, available in various styles from historic to contemporary designs. This diversity allows buyers ample choices. 
         

    
         Furthermore, Houston’s commercial real estate market is robust, featuring numerous office buildings, retail centers, and industrial spaces, meeting the demands of businesses and investors. Houston’s commitment to providing versatile and thriving real estate options reflects its dedication to a vibrant living and business environment.
      
    
      
    Property Prices
    
      
    Houston’s property prices are competitive, offering affordability across a spectrum of budgets when compared to neighboring cities. Whether you’re seeking a budget-friendly starter home or a luxurious estate, Houston provides housing options to accommodate various financial plans. 
         

    
         Despite the differing price points, residents enjoy access to exceptional amenities, world-class healthcare facilities, renowned educational institutions, and a thriving job market. Houston’s real estate market underscores its dedication to providing diverse housing options while maintaining a high quality of life for its residents.
      
    
      
    Houston Real Estate Profile – by the Numbers!
      

    In April 2026, Houston, Texas, the average home price is $497,028 and the price per square foot is $207. Average rent for a house is around $2,317 for a 3 bedrooms home.

    Cost of Living – How Affordable Living in Houston, Texas, Is
    
      
    The cost of living in Houston is slightly lower than the national average, with housing and healthcare costs being below average. Other expenses, such as utilities and groceries, are roughly on par with the national average. While transportation costs can be higher in Houston due to the city’s reliance on cars, the absence of state income tax can help offset these expenses.
      
    
      
    Reasons for Relocation – Why You Should Move to Houston, Texas
    
      
    
         With myriad career opportunities, affordable accommodation, wonderful recreational offerings, rich cultural heritage, a lively and welcoming atmosphere, and an incredibly low cost of living, Houston is an attractive destination for those seeking to relocate to a place with everything. The city’s proximity to the scenic beaches of Galveston is another reason people want to move here.  
         

    
         Eager to embrace the vibrant energy of Houston, Texas? Click here to find Houston homes for sale and rent and find an agent who works in and is familiar with Houston neighborhoods.

							Search results will be refreshed automatically when moving/zooming to match the visible area of the map.
						
    
						
    
							
							
							Filters such as Zip Code, Subdivisions, MLS Location, MLS Area and School District will be ignored when moving/zooming the map.

    Seller Contribution
    
		        
		      
    
		      
    
		        
		        
    
			        
    Seller May Contribute to Buyer Expenses
    Up To     .
    
			          
			        Contact Agent
              		        
    
		        
		        
    
					
    This reflects a seller’s willingness to consider a concession/contribution toward a buyer’s expenses but is not a guarantee. Buyer and seller may negotiate a contribution, if any, in relation to the purchase contract. Any contribution from the seller may be used by the buyer at their discretion for items like closing costs, repair expenses, interest rate buy-down, etc.

    Monthly Payment Assumptions
    
        
      
    
      
    
        
		 
		
								
			                
               
				
		
		
    
		
    Displayed Estimates include property tax, estimated home owners insurance and HOA dues/maintenance where possible. This is only an estimate. Your actual rate, payment, and costs could be higher or lower.
    
      
    
      
    
        
        
      
    
    
    
  
    

    


    
  
    
    
    
      
    
        
    Payment Estimation
    
        
      
    
      
    
	  	
    
	  	
    
			
    
			
    	  	
			
     $
    
			
    
		
    
		
    
			
    
				
    Monthly Payment Estimate Breakdown
    
				
    		     
					
    
						
    
								
    Mortgage
    
								
    
						
    
						
    
							
    Tax
    
							
    
						
    
						
    
							
    Maint. / HOA
    
							
    
						
    
						
						
    
							
    Insurance
    
							
    
						
    
					
    
				
    	
				
    						
			
    
		
    		
								
		
     
		
    Displayed estimates include property tax, estimated home owners insurance and HOA dues/maintenance where possible. This is only an estimate. Your actual rate, payment, and costs could be higher or lower. Information from Monthly Payment needs to be independently verified and not relied upon since the figure provided could be based on inaccurate presumptions.
